title: MCP Store Contract Runtime Follow-up
description: Deep-check store-aware MCP contract promises vs actual schemas/services/tests across recall, sections, extract, review, archive, and store tools. Find only new distinct issues not already in registry, especially adjacent runtime/schema/test mismatches around provenance/global_ref/written_to_store/store aliasing.
findings: 发现 2 个新 distinct issue：global_ref 的 UUID schema 与 alias-based runtime/test 冲突；fab_archive_scan 被合同误归类为会写 store 的工具。
issues_found: [{"title":"global_ref schema requires store UUID while runtime and tests use store aliases as the qualified reference","severity":"medium","description":"The canonical global_ref contract says MCP recall/cite must surface <store_uuid>[:<uid>]:<local_id>, enforced by globalRefSchema. Runtime cross-store recall instead builds ids as <alias>:<stableId>, plan-context copies those alias ids into a field named global_ref for ranking, and plan-context tests assert global_ref values like team:KT-DEC-0001. This is distinct from missing global_ref fields: even if the promised field is added, current runtime/test conventions would violate the exported schema and make store-qualified citation validation inconsistent across UUID and alias forms.","location":"packages/shared/src/schemas/store-stable-id.ts:50","fix_direction":"Choose one contract and apply it end-to-end: either make global_ref strictly UUID-based by emitting buildGlobalRef(...) from cross-store recall/plan-context and keep alias as a separate display field, or rename alias-prefixed ids to a separate store_qualified_id/cite_ref schema that explicitly accepts aliases. Add runtime contract tests that parse real plan-context/recall outputs through the exported schema.","affected_components":["packages/shared/src/schemas/store-stable-id.ts","packages/server/src/services/cross-store-recall.ts","packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts","packages/server/src/services/plan-context-scope-rank.test.ts"],"tags":["mcp-contract","global_ref","store-aliasing","schema-runtime-drift"]},{"title":"fab_archive_scan is marked as a store-writing MCP tool although the registered runtime is read-only","severity":"medium","description":"MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS classifies fab_archive_scan as echoesWrittenStore=true and comments say WRITE tools fab_archive_scan/fab_extract_knowledge echo written_to_store. The actual archive_scan schema only returns ledger scan fields (anchor_ts, session_ids, dropped, covered_through_ts, already_proposed_keys), its annotations set readOnlyHint/idempotentHint, and the service only reads events.jsonl. This is not just a missing written_to_store field: the contract assigns a write-store promise to a tool that intentionally performs no store write, so clients may wait for or audit a write target that cannot exist.","location":"packages/shared/src/schemas/mcp-store-contracts.ts:73","fix_direction":"Remove fab_archive_scan from echoesWrittenStore/write-tool expectations, or redefine the archive flow contract so only fab_extract_knowledge/fab_review write actions echo written_to_store. Add a parity test that compares MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS roles against each tool's annotations/output schema so readOnlyHint tools cannot be classified as write echoes.","affected_components":["packages/shared/src/schemas/mcp-store-contracts.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts","packages/server/src/tools/archive-scan.ts","packages/server/src/services/archive-scan.ts","packages/shared/test/store/mcp-store-contracts.test.ts"],"tags":["mcp-contract","written_to_store","archive-scan","schema-runtime-drift"]}]

title: Write Routing Metadata Follow-up
description: Deep-check active_project, write_routes, visibility_store, semantic_scope, counters, idempotency, pending/canonical path behavior across extract/review/archive/import. Find new round-trip or metadata consistency failures beyond ISS-20260609-026.
findings: 发现 3 个新 distinct 问题：Windows 路径可让 pending modify-layer 绕过 approve gate；单次写入多次解析 write-target 可在并发 route/switch 时拆分 path/visibility/counters；fab_review 只看当前 write-target pending，切换写库后旧 pending 会从 review 队列消失。
issues_found: [{"title":"Windows store paths let pending modify-layer bypass the approve gate","severity":"high","description":"modifyLayerFlip intends to reject layer flips on pending entries before allocating a stable id and writing canonical output, but the guard checks target.absPath.includes(\"/pending/\") while store pending paths on Windows use backslashes. A pending path returned by list/seed can therefore enter the layer-flip path, allocate a KP/KT id, write a canonical file, unlink the pending source, and emit layer-change events instead of the normal approve audit path.","location":"packages/server/src/services/review.ts:932","fix_direction":"Use path-aware detection instead of a POSIX substring, for example normalize target.absPath with toPosixPath/path.relative or infer the area from resolved store roots; add a Windows-shaped regression that modify-layer on knowledge\\pending\\<type> throws and leaves counters/canonical files unchanged.","affected_components":["fab_review modify-layer","review layer flip","pending/canonical promotion","Windows store paths"],"tags":["write-routing-metadata","review","path-separator","approve-gate","round-trip-invariants"]},{"title":"Write paths re-resolve write-target metadata instead of using one target snapshot","severity":"medium","description":"extractKnowledge first resolves the pending base, then later calls resolveWriteScopeMeta which reloads project/store config and resolves the target again; review approve likewise allocates counters from resolveWriteTargetStoreDir and then computes the canonical destination with resolveStoreCanonicalBase. If store switch/route config changes between those calls, a single write can persist the file in one store while stamping visibility_store or advancing counters in another store.","location":"packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:336; packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:359; packages/server/src/services/review.ts:607; packages/server/src/services/review.ts:620; packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60","fix_direction":"Resolve the effective write target once per operation and pass a target object containing store dir, alias/uuid, semantic_scope, visibility_store, and counter dir through pending render / approve / layer-flip. Add a concurrent switch-write or injected resolver-change regression asserting path, visibility_store, and counters all reference the same store.","affected_components":["fab_extract_knowledge","fab_review approve","fab_review modify-layer","cross-store-write","store counters","visibility_store"],"tags":["write-routing-metadata","store-routing","visibility-store","counters","race-consistency"]},{"title":"fab_review strands pending entries outside the current write-target store","severity":"medium","description":"The shared store core exposes aggregatePendingAcrossStores as the pending aggregation API for fab_review, but review.ts builds list/search/approve roots only from resolveStorePendingBase for the current team/personal write targets, and its sandbox whitelist only admits the currently resolved canonical roots. After switch-write or route changes, pending entries already written into a still-bound former write store are no longer listed or approvable by their absolute pending_path.","location":"packages/shared/src/store/core.ts:186; packages/server/src/services/review.ts:173; packages/server/src/services/review.ts:377; packages/server/src/services/review.ts:520","fix_direction":"Make review pending discovery and sandboxing use the mounted read/write store set or aggregatePendingAcrossStores with provenance, while preserving a stricter write-target check only for new writes. Add a two-store integration test: extract/list in store A, switch-write to store B, then verify the store A pending item remains listable and approvable by returned path.","affected_components":["fab_review list","fab_review approve","pending aggregation","store switch-write","read/write store routing"],"tags":["write-routing-metadata","pending-path","review","store-switch","round-trip-invariants"]}]

title: Related Graph Retrieval Follow-up
description: Deep-check related graph frontmatter parsing, store-qualified/bare ids, redirects, include_related, fabric-connect/review generated edges, and knowledge graph docs/tests. Find new issues beyond ISS-20260609-027.
findings: 发现 3 个新 distinct related-graph 问题：store-qualified personal 边绕过 KT→KP 隐私过滤、fabric-connect/archive 宣称的 review 写边路径实际缺失、related 扩展未应用 layer-flip redirect。
issues_found: [{"title":"Store-qualified personal related IDs bypass the KT→KP topology-leak guard","severity":"high","description":"The graph privacy guard promises to strip team-source related edges that target personal KP entries, but it calls parseKnowledgeId(targetId) directly. Current skills and cross-store recall use store-qualified IDs such as personal:KP-GLD-0001, and parseKnowledgeId only accepts bare KP-/KT- IDs, so decoded becomes null and the edge is allowed through unchanged. A team canonical entry can therefore persist a pointer to a personal knowledge node, violating the §4 privacy iron law and leaking personal graph topology.","location":"packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1107","fix_direction":"Normalize related targets before layer checks: accept alias-qualified/global-ref forms by extracting the trailing local KP-/KT- id, then run the KT→KP guard; add regression tests for related: [personal:KP-...] and bare KP targets, and align fabric-archive/fabric-connect examples with the accepted canonical form.","affected_components":["knowledge-meta-builder related frontmatter parser","fabric-archive related-edge authoring","fabric-connect graph edges","cross-store recall privacy boundary"],"tags":["related-graph-retrieval","privacy","store-qualified-id","frontmatter"]},{"title":"fabric-connect/archive cannot persist related edges through fab_review despite documenting that path","severity":"medium","description":"fabric-connect says to write related edges via the fabric-review modify path, and fabric-archive says session_context related lines survive to approve-time frontmatter authoring, but the actual FabReview modify schema only permits title/summary/layer/maturity/tags/relevance_* and review.ts only rewrites those fields. extractKnowledge also has no related input and renderFreshEntry never emits related. The documented graph-generation workflow therefore has no supported MCP write surface for canonical related frontmatter unless an agent hand-edits files, which the skill forbids.","location":"packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:884","fix_direction":"Add related?: string[] to FabReview modify changes, service ModifyChanges, rewriteFrontmatterMerge/appendPatchLines, parse/list/search output as needed, with validation for existing stable IDs and KT→KP privacy; or update skills to a real supported write path if review should not own graph edits.","affected_components":["FabReviewInputSchema","review modify service","fabric-connect skill","fabric-archive skill","related frontmatter authoring"],"tags":["related-graph-retrieval","fabric-connect","fabric-review","schema-runtime-drift"]},{"title":"Related-edge expansion ignores knowledge_id_redirect mappings after layer flips","severity":"medium","description":"recall rewrites only caller-supplied input.ids through loadIdRedirectMap before intersection, while include_related reads candidate.description.related raw and compares those raw IDs to current candidate IDs. planContext does the same related append before loading/surfacing redirects. If a related target was layer-flipped, review emits knowledge_id_redirect, but a stored related edge to the prior ID no longer expands or nudges even when the new ID is in the active candidate set.","location":"packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:97","fix_direction":"Apply the redirect map to related targets in both recall include_related and planContext include_related before candidate-set lookup, and add tests where A.related points to an old stable ID whose new ID is surfaced after modify-layer.","affected_components":["fab_recall include_related","planContext include_related","id-redirect","fabric-review modify-layer","knowledge graph retrieval"],"tags":["related-graph-retrieval","redirects","include_related","layer-flip"]}]

title: Release Perf Gate Follow-up
description: Deep-check rc gates, perf benchmark, doctor check counts, coverage thresholds, package scripts, CI workflow, docs/TESTING, and gate labels for stale or misleading contracts beyond existing ISS-20260609-012/030/031.
findings: 发现 1 个新 distinct issue：tag release 工作流复用 build/typecheck/lint/coverage/NO_COLOR gate，但漏跑普通 CI 已启用的 perf p95 gate，发布可绕过 CLI/hook 冷启动性能门禁。
issues_found: [{"title":"Release workflow omits the CI perf benchmark gate before publish","severity":"medium","description":"The normal CI validate job runs scripts/perf-benchmark.mjs as a CLI + hook cold-start p95 gate and fails on budget breaches, but the tag-triggered release workflow stops after coverage, protected-token lint, and NO_COLOR snapshots before allowing publish. A direct release tag can therefore publish packages without the same performance gate, even though the benchmark script is an explicit failing gate. This is distinct from ISS-20260609-030: the fixture may be weak, but release.yml does not run the gate at all.","location":".github/workflows/release.yml:51","fix_direction":"Add the same perf benchmark step used in .github/workflows/ci.yml before the release ci job completes, preferably uploading perf-report.json on always(); keep the gate command centralized so CI and release cannot drift.","affected_components":[".github/workflows/release.yml",".github/workflows/ci.yml","scripts/perf-benchmark.mjs","release publish gate"],"tags":["release-perf-gates","perf-gate","release-workflow","ci-drift"]}]

title: Legacy Surface Drift Follow-up
description: Deep-check quarantined HTTP/server exports/package metadata/docs/templates for legacy co-located .fabric knowledge, removed API references, obsolete CLI names, or stale generated surfaces beyond ISS-20260609-021/024.
findings: 发现 2 个新 distinct legacy-surface drift：发布版 server README 漏列现行 MCP 工具，quarantined HTTP README 仍描述已过期的 Part 2/CI 布局；已排除 ISS-20260609-021/024、ISS-20260608-046/059/006 等同类已登记问题。
issues_found: [{"title":"Published server README omits the registered fab_archive_scan MCP tool","severity":"medium","description":"packages/server/README.md 的 ‘Tools exposed’ 列表只列 fab_plan_context、fab_get_knowledge_sections、fab_recall、fab_extract_knowledge、fab_review 五个工具，遗漏当前 server 实际注册并在根 README/runtime contracts/index tests 中声明的 fab_archive_scan。由于 packages/server/README.md 会随 @fenglimg/fabric-server 发布，直接消费 server 包的集成方会拿到不完整的 MCP tool surface。","location":"packages/server/README.md:5; packages/server/src/index.ts:221; docs/RUNTIME-CONTRACTS.md:47","fix_direction":"把 packages/server/README.md 的工具清单同步到当前 6-tool surface，补入 fab_archive_scan，并考虑用同一测试或生成源校验 README/package docs 与 packages/server/src/index.ts 注册表一致。","affected_components":["packages/server/README.md","@fenglimg/fabric-server published docs","MCP tool surface docs"],"tags":["legacy-surface-drift","published-docs","mcp-contract","tool-surface-drift"]},{"title":"Quarantined HTTP README still describes the pre-Part-2 layout and workspace skip model","severity":"low","description":"packages/server-http-experimental/README.md 仍说 HTTP server files are ‘planned Part 2’ and ‘still present in packages/server/src’, while the experimental package already contains src/http.ts, middleware/bearer-auth.ts and services/serve-lock.ts. The same README also says the dir is technically a workspace member skipped by no-op scripts, but the package tsconfig documents that it is excluded from pnpm-workspace and has a standalone typecheck path. Future web UI restoration or CI triage will follow stale package-layout instructions, distinct from the already registered removed-export import drift in ISS-20260609-021.","location":"packages/server-http-experimental/README.md:20; packages/server-http-experimental/README.md:43; packages/server-http-experimental/README.md:47; packages/server-http-experimental/tsconfig.json:2","fix_direction":"Refresh the quarantine README to current post-Part-2 state: list files now owned by packages/server-http-experimental, describe the standalone typecheck/explicit exclusion model, and update restoration steps to start from the package-local sources instead of pretending the HTTP implementation still lives in packages/server/src.","affected_components":["packages/server-http-experimental/README.md","packages/server-http-experimental/tsconfig.json","HTTP quarantine restoration docs"],"tags":["legacy-surface-drift","server-http-quarantine","docs-drift","ci-surface-drift"]}]
